title: Support named volumes?
body:
Is there any thought to supporting named volumes?  Specifically
`podman run -v foo:/tmp blah`
`podman volume ls/rm/create`

I wouldn't expect podman to support volume drivers or anything such as that (unless CSI is usable, but let's ignore that).  All I would expect is that a named volume is essentially an alias for a host path.  So `foo` is really just `/var/lib/container/volumes/foo`.  I know you can say that you don't need the syntactical sugar for bind mounts, but I think it's really quite nice.  Named volumes allow you too logically manage buckets of host storage.

comments.body: We would not support using Daemons for these services. but I could see support for this short cut. I don't see this as a focus of the team at this point.  But I am sure we would accept patches.

My question would be how we would store the list of named volumes. It's not a libpod-level concept, so podman would have to find a temporary directory to dump it in. Maybe an artifacts API for libpod as a whole (similar to the per-container one) would help

I think this would be difficult to contribute without some guidance.  The issue is that doing this suddenly creates shared assets that have to be refcounted or managed in some way.  So it wouldn't be straight forward.

I am going through old Issues, trying to figure out if they are still relevant.  On this one, @ibuildthecloud is this still something you are interested in?  Would this be just a directory on disk that would be handled sort of the way we handle builtin volumes in container images.

I am fine with adding this functionality and allowed to experiment.  Should there be support for quota on these volumes or just force admins to handle it.
@umohnani8 Is this something you would like to play with?
@rhatdan sure, volumes seem to never leave me :D

Hello!
It would indeed be really, really useful in order to get neat and clean run command. It also allows a better readable documentation about the containers.
Oh, and this would also add a better compatibility layer with docker :).

Please, please, give some time to that feature :+1: 

Thank you!
@cjeanner Thanks, @umohnani8 is working on this now.
Is there progress on this feature?

I believe Urvashi is getting close to finishing a patch to add this.

On Mon, Nov 19, 2018, 06:42 Valentin Rothberg <notifications@github.com
wrote:

Yup actively working on on this.
We now have the volume command so I believe this is fixed.  Reopen if I am mistaken.
